projects.name: JCoroutines
projects.summary: Virtual Threads arrived in Java 21 but structured concurrency remained in preview — leaving a gap between capability and production-ready patterns. JCoroutines fills that gap: start parallel tasks, cancel as a group on failure, and set whole-operation timeouts, cleanly and predictably, with no framework overhead.

projects.name: ABCoroutines
projects.tagline: Structured concurrency for Kotlin on Java 21
projects.summary: Kotlin developers moving to Java 21 virtual threads needed a bridge between Kotlin coroutines and the new JVM thread model. ABCoroutines provides that bridge — structured concurrency primitives that feel idiomatic in Kotlin while running on virtual threads under the hood.

projects.name: NotNull4J
projects.summary: Static analysis tools like JSpecify catch nulls at compile time — but runtime boundaries still let them through. External APIs, databases, and deserialised payloads don't respect your annotations. NotNull4J extends the JSpecify ecosystem with runtime enforcement at trust boundaries, keeping internal JVM logic provably null-safe where it counts.

projects.name: Sandcastle Auth
projects.tagline: Spring Boot auth server with token governance
projects.summary: Standard auth setups are too rigid for complex testing environments or training scenarios that need fine-grained token control. Sandcastle Auth is a lightweight authorization server with scoped tokens, configurable expiry, and centralised role syncing — no vendor lock-in, runs locally or against a real database.

projects.name: SheafGate
projects.tagline: SvelteKit/Bun as a desktop app — without Electron
projects.summary: Electron bundles a full Chromium instance just to run a local web app — heavy, slow to start, and hard to secure. SheafGate is a lightweight Go binary that spawns a Bun/SvelteKit server on an ephemeral port, handles auth via a build-time UUID control endpoint and one-time passwords, then opens the browser. Full desktop app feel, zero Electron overhead.

projects.name: SheafJournal
projects.summary: Most developer tools are either Electron-bloated or cloud-dependent — both compromise performance and privacy. SheafJournal is a desktop PKM built on SheafGate. Markdown, Mermaid diagrams, full-text search, frontmatter tags, and a journal-style todo system — all local, all fast, no subscription.

projects.name: ShellGuard
projects.tagline: SSH proxy for AI agent shell access mediation
projects.summary: Giving AI agents shell access is powerful but risky — there is no standard layer for auditing, filtering, or rate-limiting what they can execute. ShellGuard sits between the agent and the shell, mediating access with configurable allow/deny rules, human-in-the-loop approval for risky commands, and a full audit trail.

projects.name: VerZanctuary
projects.tagline: Safe code snapshots before AI edits
projects.summary: AI-assisted coding moves fast — sometimes too fast. You need lightweight, low-friction checkpoints that are not full git commits. VerZanctuary creates named snapshots of your working state before handing off to an AI agent, so you can always compare, restore, and roll back cleanly without polluting your Git history.

projects.name: Robokeyboard
projects.summary: Repetitive workflows that standard software macros cannot reach — the gap between hardware events and application logic. Robokeyboard is an Arduino-based programmable keyboard with firmware written in C++ as an Arduino sketch, communicating via serial to a Kotlin control app. High-level automation triggered by hardware events.

projects.name: RoboKeyTags
projects.tagline: Structured comment system for AI agent communication
projects.summary: AI agents reading codebases have no structured way to understand intent, constraints, or annotations left by the human developer. RoboKeyTags is a structured comment convention that lets developers annotate code with machine-readable context — constraints, ownership, risk level — for AI agent consumption. Language-agnostic, with Kotlin tooling.

projects.name: Puzzlegrep
projects.tagline: Structured search for code, not just text
projects.summary: Plain grep finds text — it does not understand code structure. Tracking down patterns across a large codebase means sifting through false positives or writing one-off scripts. Puzzlegrep is a TypeScript CLI that matches meaningful code structures across repositories, speeding up refactors and cross-repo audits considerably.

projects.name: Music Rhythm Timing
projects.summary: Learning musical timing is tactile — most apps explain it but do not let you feel it interactively. This Android app built with Jetpack Compose uses a Redux/MVI architecture to teach rhythmic timing through interaction rather than instruction. Released GPL-3.0 for F-Droid.
